label: Vision–language–action models

node.label: Vision–language–action models
node.definition: Vision–language–action models map visual observations and language instructions to temporally grounded actions or action tokens for embodied control.
node.why_it_matters: They connect foundation-model perception and instruction following directly to physical or simulated behavior.
limitations: Action data are expensive, embodiment shifts are severe, and mistakes can create real physical consequences.

dependencies.rationale: Vision–language–action models extends or specializes the architecture family Multimodal foundation models.
Vision–language–action models extends or specializes the architecture family Latent world models & predictive dynamics.
Vision–language–action models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives.
Vision–language–action models consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
